Off Calendar Nov-19-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for William Young, Jr. Robert Bailey for Wachovia Mortgage formerly known as World Savings and now known as Wells Fargo Bank appeared by telephone.

The Court stated that Mr. Canatella listed two 5th causes of actions, that the second of the two referring to negligience will be called the 6th cause of action and that it is withdrawn as it does not pertain to the demurring party. Mr. Bailey submitted to the tentative ruling. Mr. Canatella submitted to the tentative ruling on the 2nd, 3rd and 5th causes of actions and presented oral argument to support his position with regard to the 1st cause of action. The tentative stands: 1st Cause of Action for Rescission: Tentative ruling: sustain without leave to amend based on statute of limitations. 2nd Cause of Action for Recovery of Real Property and Quiet Title: Tentative ruling: sustain with 10 days leave to amend as to statute of limitations, standing, and failure to adequately identify property at issue or Wachovia's interest in it; overrule as to Probate Code section 850 ground. 3rd Cause of Action for Cancellation of Grant Deed and Promissory Note and Deed of Trust to Wachovia and Deed of Trust to Wells Fargo: Tentative ruling: sustain with 10 days leave to amend regarding each bank as to statute of limitations, standing and no adequate facts stated; overrule as to Probate Code section 850 ground. 5th Cause of Action for Elder Abuse: Tentative ruling: sustain with 10 days leave to amend as to statute of limitations and failure to state a cause of action; overrule as to Probate Code section 850 and standing grounds.
